title: manihot anisophylla
intro: Manihot anisophylla, also known as the wild cassava or tapioca vine, is a perennial plant species in the Euphorbiaceae family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating skin conditions and wounds due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Manihot anisophylla include its ability to reduce inflammation, fight infections, and promote wound healing. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into a paste or infusion, often used topically to treat skin irritations and internally to support digestive health, although further research is necessary to confirm its efficacy.
